tags.name: Visa Payment Passkey
tags.description: This bundle encompasses the APIs intended for Merchant led flows for Visa Payment Passkey Services

post.description: Register the Attestation Response given by the FIDO2 Authenticator to the Relying Party app after the successful creation of FIDO2 credentials.
Prepare the necessary data for a subsequent Authorization Request.

The /oauth2/token endpoint is used to issue, exchange, and refresh tokens for supported authentication flows. It enables scenarios such as device authentication, authorization code to access token or ID token exchange, ID token to access token exchange, and other supported token conversion and renewal operations. This endpoint serves as the central token-processing interface for clients that need to obtain secure tokens after authentication or exchange an existing credential for another token type.
Register a new device instance with authentication keys. Returns a subject_id (device identifier) along with a linkage_url.
Verify the Assertion Response given by the FIDO2 Authenticator to the Relying Party app after a successful navigator.credentials.get() operation.

scheme.description:
The scheme of the PAN Card or Token
Values
  com_visa_pan
    A generic term that does not distinguish between card and token

secondary_id.description: The secondary id for the account

ExpiresIn.description: The expiration of the access_token in seconds

CodeChallengeMethod.description:
Describes the transformation over the code_verifier.
Mandatory if code_challenge is non-null.
- `plain`: Same as the value of code_verifier.
- `S256`: Calculated as BASE64URL-ENCODE(SHA256(ASCII(code_verifier))).

SubjectEntity.description: The type of the entity being authenticated

RedirectUri.description: Where the token(s) will be delivered to the relying party.

Probe.description: A probe is a metaphor that means that the subject will be further checked to ensure the individual in the flow is really who he/she claims to be

ServerNonce.description:
Used as a way for the relying party to participate in the generation of the FIDO2 challenge
The nonce is used as an input to creating the challenge

AuthSourceHint.description:
Source hint based on the confinement type.
- for Click to Pay use VXO
- for VTS integration use VTS
- for Cardinal use CRD
- for issuer integration use CARD_ISSUER
- for ICM use ICM

DeviceChallenge.description:
A transformation over a transitory secret, known as code_verifier, needed to prove that the same software instance that initiated the flow is the same software instance that is using the outputted token(s).
- Length: 43..128 (plain)
- Length: 43 (S256)

ActForObject.description: Non-standard OIDC parameter to identify on behalf of relationships. The act_for parameter is used when the caller is the actor who is performing a task on behalf of some other entity

ActForEntity.description: The type of the entity being authenticated

bindings.description: The bindings associated to the Resource(s) and Confinement(s) for the incoming authorization detail

isVPPSEligible.description: If the account is eligible for VPPS binding

BindingsByAuthorizationDetail.description:
Existing bindings associated to the Resource(s) and Confinement(s) for each of the elements in the incoming authorization details array
Mandatory if the include param specifies any binding data type

masked_id.description: The last 4 digits of the Masked PAN Card or Token

currency_exponent.description:
Indicates the implied position of the decimal point from the right of the transaction amount
For example, there are 100 cents in a USD, that is10 to the power of 2, so the exponent for the US dollar is 2

ID.description:
The identifier of the FIDO2 credential
The base64Url encoded value of the FIDO2 Credential ID that is being registered.
This is the identifier of the FIDO2 credential that is being registered.

SourceImgIdHint.description: Source image id hint based on the confinement type.

PARConfinements.description: The confinements associated to the PAR request

ProbeTargetCount.description: The number of probes that need to be settled for completing the authentication

Status.description: Its value is always 'ok' on this context

ActForIdType.description: The Act for id type of the entity being authenticated

SourceIdMaskedHint.description: Source ID Masked hint based on the confinement type.

top_origin.description:
The top origin. With SPC, this might be used as the 'payeeOrigin'
This origin must be found in the origin performing the fido2 authentication
at, window.location.ancestorOrigins (typically it should be the last element in the array)

software_id.description: A brief identifier for the software.

declared_origin.description: The origin to which Visa is offering a service in this session.

app_id.description: If the tenancy is specific to an application within the product.

client_id.description: If the tenancy is specific to a client within the product.

tenancy_context.description:
Helps in resolving the tenancy on which this session will operate.
This is the software JWKS that is being used to exchange the authorization code for an access token.

integrator_origin.description:
The integrator origin.
Needed to post messages, and to validate on the server whether we are ok with
posting messages to this origin
If null, the integrator_origin is the same as top_origin
TBD: This origin must be found in the origin performing the fido2 authentication
      at, window.location.ancestorOrigins (typically it should be the last element in the array)
      In some situations this might not be true. The authentication origin might be
          directly embedded in the top_origin and effectively be a sibling of the integrator_origin,
          which would not be included in this list

SoftwareStatementClaimset.description: A description of the client software that will be used within the session

SourceUserId.description: Another id for the confinement that is not created or managed by the binding system.

SigDeviceDynamicData.description:
The content of this field is a JSON Web Signature (JWS). 
Required for Digital Authentication Framework (DAF). Not needed when the signature is generated by the FIDO device.
1. The header of the JWS has these elements:
  “alg”:“...”—Algorithm used for signature
  “kid”:“1234”—Token requestors’s client device ID
  “typ”:“JOSE”
  “cty”:“application/json”—Content type of the content inside JWS
  “iat”:“147...38”—Issued at the timestamp in UTC when the transaction was created and signed.
2. Construct the body as JSON consisting of these:
  vProvisionedTokenID
  the nonce
  clientDeviceID
3. The Signature. When the signature is provided, use this algorithm: RSA-PSS with SHA-256 as the hash and the MGF. Set “algo” to “PS256”

Code.description:
The code parameter as defined by OIDC
+ The code parameter is used to exchange for an access token
+ The code is a one-time use token

SourceRootOfTrustData.description: This Data Come from Token Requestor or Token Service Provider for PAR request with wallet account ids and

ServerAuthData.description: Mandatory if the include param specifies one or more data types to return.

TransactionTokenType.description: The type of the transaction token.

ProbeUse.description:
+ Provides the client with information regarding the usage of probe data
+ Optional - the client can decide which probe to satisfy
+ Recommended - the Open ID server recommends the satisfaction of this probe, but client can decide
+ Required - the client has to satisfy the probe

ResponseMode.description:
Blank space separated list of identifiers for how the requested token is to be handed over to the caller of this API
  Values
  urn:ext:oauth:response-mode:body
<N/A> fragment: Not supported by this grant type
<N/A> query: Not supported by this grant type
<N/A> form_post: Not supported by this grant type
<N/A> urn:ext:oauth:response-mode:ua_inspector: Not supported by this grant type
<N/A> urn:ext:oauth:response-mode:ua_script: Not supported by this grant type
  urn:ext:oauth:response-mode:cookie
IMPORTANT: Cookies come at the expense of CSRF attacks. At this point in time, only secured_id_tokens are allowed to be sent back as cookies
  This array may be of
  Length 1 in which case the same way to hand over all the tokens applies
  Same length of the response_types array in which case index 0 of the response_mode array refers to the index 0 of the response_types array and so on
  Less length than the response_types array in which case the last response_mode is applicable to the rest of the response_types
  Greater length than the response_types array in which case the extra response_mode entries in the array are ignored
  Defaults to "urn:ext:oauth:response-mode:body"

Resources.description: The resources associated to the binding

ProbeData.title: Probe data
ProbeData.description:
Data for each probe is represented in this object
  + ref or type will be present which can uniquely identify the probe
  + ref or type must be sent for subsequent interactions for settling the probe.

ProbeDataValue.description:
+ Value of probe reference

Value of the Id that uniquely defines the relationship who is authenticated
 For example in case of authentication using RelationshipID

ConfinementSourceHint.description: Source hint based on the confinement type.

rp.description: The relying party responsible for the request

pubKeyCredParams.description: Preferences about the credential key to be created Ordered from most preferred to least

alg.description:
The algorithm of the public key credential.
This is the algorithm of the public key credential that is being registered.
Values
  -8 → (EdDSA)
      crv is Ed25519
      is compressed
      EdDSA using Ed25519
  -7 → (ES256)
      crv is 1 → P-256
      ECDSA using P-256 and SHA-256
      is not compressed
  -257 → (RS256)
    RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 using SHA-256

type.description:
The type of the public key credential.
This is the type of the public key credential that is being registered.

SourceId.description: Another id for the confinement that is not created or managed by the binding system.

SourceUserIdHint.description:
The type of id that identifies the user
Values
  ACCOUNT_ID

Challenge.description:
A base64url encoded randomized byte string that is to be signed by the authenticator with
The attestation key (associated to an authenticator), during a registration
The credential key (associated to an end-user), during an authentication
According to the Webauthn Spec, this value must have at least 16 bytes of entropy (2^128 bits)
16 random bytes encoded in base64url makes 22 bytes, but it is still only 2^128 bits of entropy
According to the FIDO2 Server Certification documentation, this value must be at most 64 bytes long
Before this value is passed to the FIDO2 authenticator, it must be base64url decoded. This decoded value is the actual challenge given to the authenticator

SourceImgHint.description: Source image hint based on the confinement type.

Protocol.description: If the authentication was delegated to some other party, what protocol was used

TransactionConfiguration.description: The configuration to capture token type and DAF indicators for issuer

Describes the transformation over the code_verifier.
Mandatory if code_challenge is non-null.
- `plain`: Same as the value of code_verifier.
- `S256`: Calculated as BASE64URL-ENCODE(SHA256(ASCII(code_verifier))).

(Conditional) Token Requestor Id, which identifies the owner of the token.
Format: Numberic, 11
Conditionality: Required for Token for Token use-case or deferred authentication flows.

AuthorizationEndpoint.description: Used by the relying party to redirect the end-user to the identity provider to obtain authorization to access the requested resources.

ClientWalletAccountId.description:
Client-provided consumer ID that identifies the wallet account holder entity. 
Format: Alphabetic, numeric, hyphens ( - ), and underscores ( _ ), e.g. spaces are not allowed; maximum 24 characters.

Type.description:
The type of the FIDO2 credential
The type of the FIDO2 credential that is being registered.
This value is always 'public-key'

VPPSFIDO2ServerStandardError.title: VPPS FIDO2 Server Standard Error Response Structure

errorMessage.description: The message for the error Must be the same value as 'error_description'

VPPSFIDO2ServerStandardError.description: API errors are reported using a Standard Error Response structure. All errors are categorized into Standard errors and Business errors. Standard errors can be returned by any API and should be handled in a common way. Business errors are returned only by some APIs and are documented for each API.

Confinements.description: The confinements associated to the binding

UILocales.description:
The UI Locales that the user has selected
The UI Locales are the languages that the user has selected to use for

AttestationObject.description:
The attestation object
The base64Url encoded value of the attestation object that is being registered.
This is the attestation object that is being registered.
It's a structured value as per the WebAuthn specification
It consists of the following
  fmt: the format type in which the attestation is expressed
  attStmt: the attestation statement
    Its contents are specific to the format being used
    Always includes the attestation signature
authData: the authenticator data
    rpIdHash: the sha-256 hash of the rpId (the origin)
    flags: defining bit flags for user present, user verified, backup eligibility (0 - single, 1 - multi device), backup state (0 - not backed up, 1 -backed up), attested credential data present, and extension data present
    signCount: the signature counter (increases with each signature)
   attestedCredentialData
      aaguid: the AAGUID of the authenticator
      credentialIdLength: byte length of the credential id
      credentialId: the FIDO2 credential id
      credentialPublicKey: the COSE encoded public key
   extensions: map with extension identifiers as keys and authenticator extension outputs as values

residentKey.description:
The extent to which a client-side discoverable credential is desired.
Values:
- discouraged
- preferred
- required
Defaults to required when requireResidentKey is true.
Defaults to discouraged when requireResidentKey is false or absent.

requireResidentKey.description:
Whether a resident key is desired.
Retained only for backward compatibility with webauthn level 1.
Defaults to false.
Mapping between residentKey and requireResidentKey:
- residentKey = discouraged → requireResidentKey = false
- residentKey = required → requireResidentKey = true
- residentKey = preferred → requireResidentKey = false (not clear)

AuthenticatorSelectionCriteria.description: Specifies requirements for the authenticator that affect how the FIDO2 credential is created.

Prompt.description:
Specifies how the end-user must be involved during the authentication process.
Its value is a space-delimited string.
Possible values include:
- 'login': The end-user must be actively authenticated.
- 'create`: The end-user is to be enrolled.
- 'none': The end-user is authenticated silently, without their active participation (not implemented).
- 'consent': The end-user must consent before returning any information to the Client (not implemented).
- 'select_account': The end-user might select an account if multiple sessions with different accounts are already available (not implemented).

CodeChallenge.description:
A transformation over a transitory secret, known as code_verifier, needed to prove that the same software instance that initiated the flow is the same software instance that is using the outputted token(s).
- Length: 43..128 (plain)
- Length: 43 (S256)

AccessibleFromHint.description: Defaults to UNKNOWN if not present.

ErrorMessage.description: An empty string as there is no error

RelatedId.description:
The relying party identifier.
A domain string identifying the relying party for which the FIDO2 credential is created
  The FIDO2 credential will be scoped to this id
Defaults to the origin's domain
  It can only be overridden to a suffix of the origin's domain
  For example, if the browser's origin is 'secure.checkout.visa.com' then the value of rp.id might be
    secure.checkout.visa.com (default, when rp.id = null)
    checkout.visa.com
    visa.com

ProbeType.description:
The type of probe being performed on the subject
+ PROVE_PROMISE
  + The OIDC flow, by presenting a login_hint, is claiming to have interacting in the flow with the individual identified by the login_hint value
  + That is another metaphor and is called a promise; the subject needs to prove the promise made

UserVerification.description:
The user verification of the authenticator.
This is the user verification of the authenticator that is being requested.
Values
  - required
  - preferred
  - discouraged
  - default

Name.description: The name attribute takes a traditional username or the like, to disambiguate accounts with similar displayNames Authenticators must allow at least a length of 64 bytes (more than that might be truncated)

RPName.description:
The human-readable name of the relying party.
This is the name of the relying party that is requesting the FIDO2 credential.
Authenticators must allow at least a length of 64 bytes (more than that might be truncated)

IDTokens.description: ID Tokens are used to authenticate the user and provide access to the user's profile information.

type.description: Type of the Fido2 Key. Possible values include public-key, strong-key.

user_handle.description:
The user handle
This is the user handle that is being registered.

authenticator_data.description:
The authenticator data
The base64Url encoded value of the authenticator data that is being registered.
This is the authenticator data that is being registered.
It's a structured value as per the WebAuthn specification
It consists of the following
  rpIdHash: the sha-256 hash of the rpId (the origin)
  flags: defining bit flags for user present, user verified, backup eligibility (0 - single, 1 - multi device), backup state (0 - not backed up, 1 -backed up), attested credential data present, and extension data present
  signCount: the signature counter (increases with each signature)
  extensions: map with extension identifiers as keys and authenticator extension outputs as values

assertion_signature.description:
The signature
 This is the signature that is being registered.

BindingsByAuthorizationDetails.description:
Existing bindings associated to the Resource(s) and Confinement(s) for each of the elements in the incoming authorization details array
Mandatory if the include param specifies any binding data type

UUID.description: The unique id of the confinement created by the binding system.

Subjects.description: This is an array of all the subjects being authenticated in this interaction

SessionId.description:
The session identifier.
This is the identifier of the session that is being created.

AMR_VALUES.description:
The authentication methods being requested.
Note: For VPPS only `pop#fido2` is used.
Values from OIDC:
- `pop`: Proof of Possession. Similar to swk and hwk without specifying if it is through software or hardware.
Custom Values:
- `fed`: Federated.
These values might be qualified for more granularity:
Qualifier Values:
- When `amr` is 'otp':
  - `otp#time`: Time-based OTP.
  - `otp#hash`: Hash-based OTP.
  - `otp#email`: OTP via email.
  - `otp#sms`: OTP via SMS.
  - `otp#txn`: OTP with a payment transaction amount.
- When `amr` is 'pin':
  - `pin#cvv`: PIN with CVV.
- When `amr` is 'tel':
  - `tel#csr`: Telephone with customer service representative.
  - `tel#cb`: Telephone callback.
- When `amr` is 'rba':
  - `rba#rf`: Risk-Based Authentication with refresh token.
  - `rba#aref`: Risk-Based Authentication with activity reference.
  - `rba#sidt`: Risk-Based Authentication with secured ID token.
  - `rba#csrt`: Risk-Based Authentication with cross-site recognition token.
  - `rba#tpt`: Risk-Based Authentication with third-party token.
- When `amr` is 'pop':
  - `pop#fido2`: Proof of Possession with FIDO2.
- When `amr` is 'fed':
  - `fed#oidc`: Federated with OpenID Connect protocol.
  - `fed#tds`: Federated with 3DS protocol.
  - `fed#saml2`: Federated with SAML2 protocol.
  - `fed#vtsiss`: Federated with VTS IdV specification.

SubjectAuthStatus.description:
Authentication status of the subject
+ A - AUTHENTICATED
+ P - PROBE

AuthorizationDetailRef.description:
Typically, a one-digit number
A reference or a label optionally set by the creator of the authorization detail and acknowledged by B2C IAM, to uniquely refer to one element in an array of authorization details
If not specified its value is the 1-based index on the array (index starts from one)
Mandatory when a Binding data type is part of a response

AMR.description:
Authentication methods that were used during the authentication process of the user. These methods can include various types of authentication like passwords, biometrics, OTPs (One-Time Passwords), etc. The amr claim is part of the token payload and helps the relying party (RP) understand how the user was authenticated, which can impact the level of trust and security associated with the token.
Values from RFC:
- `pwd`: Password.
- `otp`: One Time Password. Without qualifier it means totp.
- `pin`: Personal Identification Number.
- `kba`: Knowledge Based Authentication.
- `swk`: Software Secured Key.
- `hwk`: Hardware Secured Key.
- `sc`: Smart Card.
- `fpt`: Fingerprint.
- `face`: Faceprint.
- `iris`: Irisprint.
- `retina`: Retinaprint.
- `vbm`: Voiceprint.
- `rba`: Risk Based Authentication.
- `user`: User Presence.
- `sms`: Short Message Service.
- `tel`: Telephone.
- `geo`: Geolocation.
- `mca`: Multiple Channel Authentication.
- `mfa`: Multiple Factor Authentication.
- `wia`: Windows Integrated Authentication.
- values from OIDC:
- `pop`: Proof of Possession. Similar to swk and hwk without specifying if it is through software or hardware.
- custom values:
- `fed`: Federated.
- When `amr` is 'otp':
  - `otp#time`: Time-based OTP.
  - `otp#hash`: Hash-based OTP.
  - `otp#email`: OTP via email.
  - `otp#sms`: OTP via SMS.
  - `otp#txn`: OTP with a payment transaction amount.
- When `amr` is 'pin':
    - `pin#cvv`: PIN with CVV.
- When `amr` is 'tel':
    - `tel#csr`: Telephone with customer service representative.
    - `tel#cb`: Telephone callback.
- When `amr` is 'rba':
    - `rba#rf`: Risk-Based Authentication with refresh token.
    - `rba#aref`: Risk-Based Authentication with activity reference.
    - `rba#sidt`: Risk-Based Authentication with secured ID token.
    - `rba#csrt`: Risk-Based Authentication with cross-site recognition token.
    - `rba#tpt`: Risk-Based Authentication with third-party token.
- When `amr` is 'pop':
    - `pop#fido2`: Proof of Possession with FIDO2.
- When `amr` is 'fed':
    - `fed#oidc`: Federated with OpenID Connect protocol.
    - `fed#tds`: Federated with 3DS protocol.
    - `fed#saml2`: Federated with SAML2 protocol.
    - `fed#vtsiss`: Federated with VTS IdV specification.

Timeout.description:
The timeout of the FIDO2 registration flow.
This is the timeout of the FIDO2 registration flow that is being requested.

UEBAS.description: The user entity behavior analytics, Mandatory if the include param specifies UEBA

ConfinementResourceName.description: A name given to the confinement.

ResponseType.description:
description: |
Tells which tokens are expected after a successful authentication. The redirect URI.
    code

scope.description:
Per the RFC, needed if the scope being granted is not exactly the same the client/caller is requesting
The OAuth2 RFC defines the scope as a list of space-delimited strings (URIs in our case)

response.description:
When this attribute is present, no other attribute must be directly declared as part of the response, but rather they must be included as part of the claimset of this JWT
Might be either a JWS or JWE
Initially only JWE is implemented

nonce.description: Might be issued when the JWT includes a key derivation method claim (key_dm)

mac_tag.description: Might be issued when the JWT includes a key derivation method claim (key_dm)

server_nonce.description: Might be issued when the JWT includes a key derivation method claim (key_dm)

tenancy_hint.description: Used in cases where disambiguating the tenancy is needed

auth_data.description:
Used to communicate any additional data that results from the authentication
Key Establishment
If the credential used to authenticate includes key establishment parameters (key_agr and key_dm), then this map is used to output parameters related to the key establishment

expires_in.description:
The number of seconds until the access token expires.
This is the number of seconds until the access token expires.

token_type.description:
Might be
  Bearer
  PoP
The server decides based on contextual data what token type to create

auth_status.description:
The authentication status.
This is the authentication status that is being returned.

access_token.description:
Only access tokens are placed here; not any other type of token.
This is the access token that is being returned.

AuthenticatorAttachment.description:
The attachment modality of the authenticator that is eligible for the registration.
Values:
        - platform
        - cross-platform
If <null> or empty then any attachment is acceptable

State.description: If the relying party would like to pass data that when the flow concludes and tokens are delivered, can be used to remember what exact instance this flow is.

SourceIdHint.description:
Source ID hint based on the confinement type.
Values:

When Type is DEVICE:
  - VDID - Visa Device ID
  - ClientDeviceID - Client-provided device identifier
  - IP_ADDRESS - IP address of the device
  - DFP_SID - Device Fingerprinting Session ID

When Type is DOMAIN_NAME:
  - HOSTNAME - Domain name used for the confinement

When Type is PAN:
  - VCARDID - Visa Card ID
  - PAYMENT_ACCOUNT_REFERENCE - Payment Account Reference (PAR) value
  - PAN_REFERENCE_ID - Unique reference to a PAN
  - ACCOUNT_ID_HASH - Hashed version of the account identifier

When protocol is TDS:
  - ACS_TNX_ID - Access Control Server Transaction ID

When protocol is OIDC:
  - IDT_JTI - JWT ID from an Identity Token

When protocol is VTS_IDV:
  - VTS_IDV - Visa Token Service Identity Verification reference

Proof.description:
If the creation or declaration of the new authentication credential, outputted any cryptographically protected object
Examples might be
  Id Token (JWS signed by Visa)
  FIDO2 Attestation / Assertion (a blob signed by the FIDO2 authenticator)

DafIndicator.description: configuration to hold factorA, factorB values

BindingTime.description: The time the binding was created. ISO 8601

AttestationConveyancePreference.description:
Standard error response body provided when there is an error.
Values
  none
    Might be used to avoid
    A user consent to give data to the relying party
    A roundtrip to an Attestation or Anonymization CA
  indirect
    The client (the browser) decides how to obtain the attestation
    The client may replace the authenticator's attestation with one generated by an Anonymization CA to preserve privacy
  direct
    The relying party prefers the client to relay the attestation as generated by the authenticator
  Defaults to 'none'

UserVerificationRequirement.description:
Whether the authenticator should locally verify the end-user in order to authorize the registration.
Values:
- required: The client (the browser) must return an error if a user verification cannot be performed.
- preferred: The relying party will accept a registration response that includes the UV flag not set.
- discouraged: Possibly to minimize disruption to the end-user.
Defaults to 'preferred'

ActForRealm.description: The entity who authenticated the relationship

ProbeDataReference.title: Type of probe Reference
ProbeDataReference.description:
A unique identifier to the probe item being attached to the authentication
 + Mandatory if type is not present

ProbeDataSpecAuthorizationDetails.description: The authorization details for the probe

Time.description:
The time the authentication took place
The use of the last authentication where the user actively participated of it is much preferred
For example, an authentication with a cookie
It is preferred, rather than the time the cookie authentication happened,
To use the time of the original authentication that resulted in the creation of the cookie

Surrogate.description:
The chain link that defines a new authentication credential that
Will replace the authentication credential described in the anchor

VProvisionTokenId.description: Token Reference Id assigned by VISA.

ProbesArray.description: This is an array of all the probes part of this authentication

anchor.description: The chain link that encompasses the justification to create the binding

TrustChain.description:
The authentication proof that justifies the creation of the authentication credential 
that will be leveraged by the binding

ConfinementSourceIdHint.description: Source ID hint based on the confinement type.

AuthSourceIdHint.description: Source ID hint based on the Authentication type. When protocol is TDS ACS_TNX_ID - Access Control Server Transaction ID IDT_JTI- Identity Token JWT ID VTS_IDV- Visa Transaction System Identity Document Verification AUTHENTICATOR_CREDENTIAL_ID - Credential ID this will be available only for the authenticator in response

Attestationformat.description:
The preference for how the attestation is formatted.
This is the attestation format that is being requested.
Values
  - packed
  - fido-u2f
  - tpm
  - android-key
  - android-safetynet
  - none

DisplayName.description:
Corresponds to the 'user.displayName' field of a webauthn option.
The displayName attribute takes a human-readable name for the user.
This name is intended only for display purposes and does not have to be unique.
Note: In this context, the reason this is a mandatory parameter is only due to the requirements for a certifiable FIDO2 Server.

SourceIdMasked.description: Masked version of the source id

parts.description: The parts of the contract detail

id.description: The value of the contract detail

uri.description: The URI of the contract detail

type.description: The label of the contract detail

items.description: The part of the contract detail

ueba_source.description: Source of the user entity behavior analytics.

RPId.description:
The relying party identifier.
A domain string identifying the relying party for which the FIDO2 credential is created
  The FIDO2 credential will be scoped to this id
Defaults to the origin's domain
  It can only be overridden to a suffix of the origin's domain
  For example, if the browser's origin is 'secure.checkout.visa.com' then the value of rp.id might be
    secure.checkout.visa.com (default, when rp.id = null)
    checkout.visa.com
    visa.com

AuthCode.description: A token that grants access only to internal APIs

ProbeDataSpec.title: Probe Data Spec
ProbeDataSpec.description:
Data specification for the probe.
+ Probe data type delivery for OTP

Transports.description:
The transports of the public key credential. This is a hint for how the client (the browser) might communicate with the authenticator
The value of this array corresponds to the 'transports' response field of the Attestation API from which this credential originated
This is the transports of the public key credential that is being excluded.
Values
  - usb
  - nfc
  - ble
  - internal
  - hybrid

SourceImgId.description: Source image id based on the confinement type.

source_id.description: What is known as device fingerprint session id.

source_ueba_id.description: The id outputted by the UEBA tool, typically known as device id.

Data.description:
Data defined by the application and opaque to IAM
If the app front-end would like to share data with the app back-end then this is the mechanism
These data will be shared to the app back-end through the use of B2C IAM Events

ProbeDataSpecAuthorizationDetail.description:
The authorization detail for the probe.
This object has two variations:
1. Transaction types (com_visa_payment_transaction, com_visa_payment_transaction_or_binding):
  The 'details' field is MANDATORY.
2. Binding types (com_visa_binding_lookup, com_visa_payment_credential_binding, com_visa_c2p_migration_and_transaction):
  The 'details' field is OPTIONAL.

ref.description:
Typically, a one-digit number

A reference or a label optionally set by the creator of the authorization detail and acknowledged by B2C IAM, to uniquely refer to one element in an array of authorization details
If not specified its value will be the 1-based index on the array (index starts from one)
Mandatory when a Binding data type is part of a responseTypically, a one-digit number
A reference or a label optionally set by the creator of the authorization detail and acknowledged by B2C IAM, to uniquely refer to one element in an array of authorization details

If not specified its value will be the 1-based index on the array (index starts from one)

Mandatory when a Binding data type is part of a response

currency.description:
The currency of the authorization
https://www.iban.com/currency-codes

currency_number.description:
Similar to currency code but with digits
https://www.iban.com/currency-codes

currency_exponent.description:
Indicates the implied position of the decimal point from the right of the transaction amount
For example, there are 100 cents in a USD, that is10 to the power of 2, so the exponent for the US dollar is 2

locale.description: The locale preferences for the authorization

email.description: The email address for the notification

phone.description: The phone number for the notification

notification.description: The notification preferences for the authorization

enduser_approval.description: The end user approval preferences for the authorization

Typically, a one-digit number

A reference or a label optionally set by the creator of the authorization detail and acknowledged by B2C IAM, to uniquely refer to one element in an array of authorization details
If not specified its value will be the 1-based index on the array (index starts from one)
Mandatory when a Binding data type is part of a responseTypically, a one-digit number
A reference or a label optionally set by the creator of the authorization detail and acknowledged by B2C IAM, to uniquely refer to one element in an array of authorization details

If not specified its value will be the 1-based index on the array (index starts from one)

Mandatory when a Binding data type is part of a response

Similar to currency code but with digits
https://www.iban.com/currency-codes

Indicates the implied position of the decimal point from the right of the transaction amount
For example, there are 100 cents in a USD, that is10 to the power of 2, so the exponent for the US dollar is 2

Subject.description: Subject is an individual or an entity that is being authenticated in this interaction

ClientDataJSON.description:
The client data JSON
The base64Url encoded value of the client data JSON that is being registered.
This is the client data JSON that is being registered.
It's a structured value as per the WebAuthn specification
    It consists of the following
       type: on a registration this value is always 'webauthn.create'
      challenge: the challenge previously generated at the server
      origin: the origin associated to the FIDO2 credential
      crossOrigin: <not sure about the meaning of this in this context>
      tokenBinding: information about the token binding protocol
        status: supported or present
        id: the base64url encoded value of the token binding id

ClientDeviceId.description:
Stable and unique device identification set by the wallet provider. By default, this field should be considered required. 
It is optional for the following presentation types: ECOM, MERCHANT_PRESENTED_QR, QR_CONSUMER_CLOUD, and RECEIVE_ONLY. 
An Enroll PAN request for a card on file will not have a device ID
Format: Alphabetic, numeric, hyphens ( - ), and underscores ( _ ), e.g. spaces are not allowed; 24 characters

SourceHint.description: Source hint based on the confinement type.

Response.description: An Authorization Request Object as a JWT in the form of a JWS, with compact serialization, signed by B2C IAM.

Scope.description: The scope of the access request. Its value is a space-delimited string. Must include the value `openid`.

SubjectRealm.description: The entity who authenticated the subject

RelationshipId.description:
The ID identifying the relationship between a token requestor and the caller of this API, which is a token service provider (TR-TSP) with which the token requestor has a relationship.
The relationship is defined at the time of onboarding; contact your Visa representative for more information.
Format: String. Alphanumeric; maximum 100 characters

warning__details.description: Array of fields - Data validation errors should use this array to specify  fields that failed validation using the location field

warning.description:
The value of this field is using an expression to point to a field that failed validation.
This field is required if error details are specified and for For non-standard error codes.

warning_description.description: The specific warning description for this field

VPPSWarning.description: This section does not exist by itself, it always accompanies either a SUCCESS or an ERROR.

SrcDigitalCardId.description: Pan Reference Id assigned by VISA. Also known as vPanEnrollmentId.

BindingStatus.description: Dictates whether the binding can be used Only ACTIVE and PENDING bindings are usable By default, a binding is created with an ACTIVE status Unless not all the necessary data to create the binding is readily available In such case, a binding is created as PENDING Values ACTIVE The binding is ready to be used Valid transitions to INACTIVE DELETED INACTIVE The use of the binding has been suspended Valid transitions to ACTIVE DELETED DELETED The binding is no longer available Valid transitions to <none> (it is a terminal state) PENDING The binding is in the process of being created Valid transitions to ACTIVE DELETED. NOT_ELIGIBLE This Payment Instrument is not Eligible for Binding.

SubjectID.description:
Id of the subject who is authenticated
+ For example in case of authentication using vCardID, the value of vCardID

VPPSNonFIDO2ServerStandardError.title: VPPS NON FIDO2 Server Standard Error Response Structure

error.description:
As this is not an OAuth2 API, the possible values must be a subset of those APIs
Values
  -invalid_request
  -invalid_client
  -server_error
  -temporarily_unavailable

The value of this field is using an expression to point to a field that failed validation.
This field is required if error details are specified and for For non-standard error codes.
example: notfound_amr_values

error_description.description: The specific error description for this field

error_details.description: Array of fields - Data validation errors should use this array to specify  fields that failed validation using the location field

error_description.description: The message for the error Must be the same value as 'error_description'

VPPSNonFIDO2ServerStandardError.description: API errors are reported using a Standard Error Response structure. All errors are categorized into Standard errors and Business errors. Standard errors can be returned by any API and should be handled in a common way. Business errors are returned only by some APIs and are documented for each API.

visaDevicePublicKey.description: The public key of the device

VPPSPushedAuthorizationResponse.description: An Authorization Request Object as a JWT in the form of a JWS, with compact serialization

example1.summary: Sample Http Response for Payment Transaction

VPPSMethodNotAllowedErrorResponse.description: 405 (Method Not Allowed) HTTP Status code indicates that this Visa Developer Center project does not have permission to use this http

VPPSForbiddenErrorResponse.description:
The 403 (Forbidden) HTTP Status code indicates that this Visa Developer Center project does not have permission to access the requested resource.
This can happen if you are trying to invoke an API request for a resource that is not part of your project.

Check for the following:
  + You are using a project and the credential for the project that includes the API being accessed.
  + You created a project and received sandbox credentials for the API’s being called.
  + You added the API being called to the project (if not already present) with the credentials that are being used.

example.error_description: API is not authorized for use

VPPSAPINoLongerSupportedError.description:
Gone - Indicates that the resource at this end point is no longer available.
This is usually returned for old API versions that are not available anymore - EOL (End-of-Life). The content-type header in the response indicates the latest supported API version.

VPPSServiceUnavailableError.description: The Visa system is currently unable to handle the request due to a temporary overloading or maintenance of the server. There may be a Retry-After header field to suggest an appropriate amount of time for the client to wait before retrying the request. The value of this field can be either an HTTP-date or a number of seconds to delay after the response is received. Retry-After = HTTP-date || delay-seconds examples: + Retry-After: Thu, 31 Dec 2020 23:59:59 GMT + Retry-After: 120 Please contact Visa Support at developer@visa.com if the issue persists.

VPPSTooManyRequestsError.description:
The Visa Developer Center project has clocked too many requests in a given amount of time.
Please contact Visa Support at developer@visa.com if you want to increase the time limits.

example.error_description: VDP clocked too many requests in a given amount of time

VPPSUnSupportedMediaTypeError.description: 415  - Indicates that the Media Type is not Supported.

VPPSNotFoundErrorResponse.description: The specified resource was not found

example.error_description: Request fields could not be validated

VPPSInputValidationErrorResponse.description: The API server could not understand the request;

VPPSInternalServerError.description:
Internal Server Error.

Please contact Visa Support at developer@visa.com with a complete error message, full http request and response details.

VPPSNotAcceptableContentError.description:
Not Acceptable - Indicates that the "Accept:" header field is present in a request and none of the available representations for the response have a media type that is listed as acceptable.
This can happen when the requested API version is not available or unsupported; for example, when the version requested in the Accept: header is newer than the currently live/supported version.

VPPSUnauthorizedErrorResponse.description:
API authorized error caused due to any of the following reasons:
+ x-pay-token is not present, blank, or not in a valid format.
+ Timestamp field not in valid UTC timestamp format.
+ Version field is invalid.
+ Token's timestamp is more than 8 minutes off from the current time.
+ Token hash mismatch.
+ APIKey not present in request.
+ APIKey is not active.
+ APIKey not valid.
+ APIKey not active.
+ Request has more than one authentication token, which is not supported

include.name: include
include.description:
The include query parameter is used to specify the additional fields that should be included in the response.
The include query parameter is optional. If the include query parameter is not provided, the response will include the default set of fields.

X-VIA-HINT.name: X-VIA-HINT
X-VIA-HINT.description:
The X-VIA-HINT header is used to provide a hint to the Visa network about the location of the client. This hint is used to optimize the routing of the request to the nearest Visa data center.
The value of the X-VIA-HINT header is a two-letter country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) that represents the country where the client is located.
The X-VIA-HINT header is optional. If the header is not provided, the Visa network will use other methods to determine the location of the client.

ACTIVITY-REF.name: activity_ref
ACTIVITY-REF.description:
The cookie must be marked "secure"
   The cookie must be marked "httponly"
   Each security token created as a cookie must be in its own cookie

DEVICE-ID.name: confinementId
DEVICE-ID.description: Visa Common device Intelligence device id

ACTIVITY-REF-SSI.name: activity_ref_ssi
ACTIVITY-REF-SSI.description:
The cookie must be marked "secure"
The cookie must be marked "httponly"
Each security token created as a cookie must be in its own cookie

X-SERVICE-CONTEXT.name: X-SERVICE-CONTEXT
X-SERVICE-CONTEXT.description: The apn enables the identification of the metadata/configuration to use while servicing this request"

X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER.name: X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER
X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER.description:
The X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER header is used to provide a hint to the Visa network about the type of session that is being used to access the API. This hint is used to optimize the routing of the request to the nearest Visa data center.
The value of the X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER header is a string that represents the type of session that is being used. The value can be one of the following:
- `IAM` - The request is being made using an IAM session.
- `NON-IAM` - The request is being made using a non-IAM session.
The X-IAM-SESSION-QUALIFIER header is optional. If the header is not provided, the Visa network will use other methods to determine the type of session that is being used.

VCARD-ID.name: resourceId
VCARD-ID.description: Virtual card id for the PAN

x-api-key.name: x-api-key
x-api-key.description: API Key for authentication provided during onboarding. (apiKey=M93HLKI2M55ST8QR9RYW3PUFG42mrpN0CsMeLNWbFWPAteAGA) required: true

BINDING-ID.name: bindingId
BINDING-ID.description: binding id of the cardholder information

CONFINEMENT-TYPE.name: confinementType
CONFINEMENT-TYPE.description: Confinement type of the cardholder information

RESOURCE-TYPE.name: resourceType
RESOURCE-TYPE.description: Resource type of the cardholder information

SERVER-AUTHORIZATION-IN-RESPONSE.description:
Used when the response is protected for integrity and authenticity
Follows the same notation as the Authorization header; for example
Server-Authorization: PoP <token>; ct="AUTHENTICATED_MESSAGE"

TRACE-HEADER-IN-RESPONSE.description: Unique ID for the API response; V-C-Client-Correlation-Id is echoed back. Used for end-to-end tracing/debugging.

RETRY-AFTER-HEADER-IN-RESPONSE.description: Header field to suggest an appropriate amount of time for the client to wait before retrying the request, typically after a 503 error or after a batch request when the batch job is not completed. The value of this field can be either an HTTP-date or a number of seconds to delay after the response is received. Retry-After = HTTP-date || delay-seconds examples: + Retry-After: Thu, 31 Dec 2020 23:59:59 GMT + Retry-After: 120
